{"credits": [{"type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/86124507?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/momenashrafff/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/momenashrafff/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/momenashrafff/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/momenashrafff/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/momenashrafff", "id": 86124507, "login": "momenashrafff", "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjg2MTI0NTA3", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/momenashrafff/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/momenashrafff/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/momenashrafff/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/momenashrafff/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/momenashrafff/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/momenashrafff", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-54307", "cvss": {"score": 9.6, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:C/C:H/I:H/A:N"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 9.6, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:C/C:H/I:H/A:N"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 8.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:N/PR:L/UI:N/VC:H/VI:N/VA:N/SC:H/SI:H/SA:L"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-863", "name": "Incorrect Authorization"}], "description": "## Impact
A member-level user with editor access to a shared workflow could reference credentials they do not own via specific public API endpoints. Credential ownership checks were only enforced partially leading to cross-user credential access.

This issue affects instances where workflow sharing is enabled and at least one workflow has been shared with a member-level user as an Editor.

## Patches
The issue has been fixed in n8n versions 1.123.55, 2.25.7, and 2.26.2. Users should upgrade to one of these versions or later to remediate the vulnerability.

## Workarounds
If upgrading is not immediately possible, administrators should consider the following temporary mitigations:
- Restrict workflow sharing to fully trusted users only.
- Audit shared workflows for unexpected credential references or recent modifications.

These workarounds do not fully remediate the risk and should only be used as short-term mitigation measures.

---
n8n has adopted CVSS 4.0 as primary score for all security advisories. CVSS 3.1 vector strings are provided for backwards compatibility.

title: n8n: Credential Exfiltration via Permission Bypass
